steps.title: Find the radius slope
steps.content:
A tangent line is perpendicular to the radius at the point of tangency. The radius goes from $(2,-5)$ to $(-2,-6)$, so its slope is
$$
\frac{-6-(-5)}{-2-2}=\frac{-1}{-4}=\frac{1}{4}
$$

steps.title: Use the tangent slope
steps.content: The tangent slope is the negative reciprocal of the radius slope. $$ -\frac{1}{\frac{1}{4}}=-4 $$ The answer is A.
